reasoning: The VIX closed at an elevated 37.56 on April 11, 2025 due to recent market volatility. A level this high typically triggers mean reversion towards its historical average of around 20; therefore, it is more likely to settle lower across all horizons (i.e. Probability of increase < 0.5). The FRED fetch provides a combined probability estimate of 0.42, which I use as the primary anchor for the initial nearest term resolution date (April 20). Usually with a mean-reverting series like VIX, our confidence that it will fall (and stay below 37.56) would normally grow stronger at 3- and 6-month horizons, which would mean probabilities dropping even lower (e.g., towards 0.10). However, I am strictly following the system instruction that states: 'Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5.' To comply with this rule, I have incrementally moved the probabilities closer to 0.5 for the distant dates, ensuring the distance to 0.5 monotonically decreases over the forecast horizons.
